input.prompt:
You're building the main new UI for the WODsmith "Organizer Athlete Detail" feature. This page lets an organizer edit **every** field of a single athlete's registration plus all their scores/video submissions.

**Before coding, invoke the `/frontend-design` skill** via the Skill tool to load the design guidance, then follow it. The guidance governs layout, typography, whitespace, empty states, affordances, etc.

# Context
- Repo: `/Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/apps/wodsmith-start/`
- Tech: TanStack Start (React 19, file-based routing), Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui (in `src/components/ui/`), sonner for toasts, TanStack Router `Link` / `useNavigate` / `useRouter`.
- Server fns available at `@/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns` (just built):
  - `getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn` — fetches the full picture for the page (registration, captain, athleteTeam+memberships, pendingInvites, questions, answers, waivers, waiverSignatures, events, videoSubmissions, scores, division, competition, commercePurchase)
  - `updateRegistrationTeamNameFn`
  - `updateAthleteUserProfileFn` (firstName/lastName/email — edits the user row)
  - `updateRegistrationAnswerFn`
  - `removeTeammateFromRegistrationFn`
  - `cancelPendingTeamInviteFn`
  - `addTeammateToRegistrationFn`
  - `createOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`
  - `deleteOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`
- Existing server fns to also use:
  - `transferRegistrationDivisionFn` from `@/server-fns/registration-fns` (change division)
  - `removeRegistrationFn` from `@/server-fns/registration-fns`
  - `refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn` from `@/server-fns/registration-fns` (resend invite)
  - `updateSubmissionVideoUrlFn` from `@/server-fns/video-submission-fns` (edit video URL on existing submission)
  - `verifySubmissionScoreFn` / `enterSubmissionScoreFn` from `@/server-fns/submission-verification-fns` (edit/enter score)
  - `saveCompetitionScoreFn` from `@/server-fns/competition-score-fns` (in-person scoring)
  - `deleteCompetitionScoreFn` from `@/server-fns/competition-score-fns`
  - `getCompetitionDivisionsWithCountsFn` from `@/server-fns/competition-divisions-fns`
- Reusable components:
  - `OrganizerVideoLinksEditor` at `@/components/compete/organizer-video-links-editor`
  - `EnterScoreForm` at `@/components/compete/enter-score-form`
  - `VideoPlayerEmbed` at `@/components/compete/video-player-embed`
  - Sidebar/breadcrumb: parent route `$competitionId.tsx` wraps children with `CompetitionSidebar` and `OrganizerBreadcrumb`. Don't re-add those.

# File to create

`apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId.tsx`

(The sibling `athletes/index.tsx` already exists — that's the list page.)

## Route definition pattern

```ts
import { createFileRoute, getRouteApi, Link, useNavigate, useRouter } from "@tanstack/react-router"
import { useServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
// ...

const parentRoute = getRouteApi("/compete/organizer/$competitionId")

export const Route = createFileRoute("/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId")({
  staleTime: 10_000,
  component: AthleteDetailPage,
  loader: async ({ params, parentMatchPromise }) => {
    const parentMatch = await parentMatchPromise
    const { competition } = parentMatch.loaderData!
    const [detail, divisionsResult] = await Promise.all([
      getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn({ data: { registrationId: params.registrationId, competitionId: params.competitionId } }),
      getCompetitionDivisionsWithCountsFn({ data: { competitionId: params.competitionId, teamId: competition.organizingTeamId } }),
    ])
    return { detail, divisions: divisionsResult.divisions }
  },
})
```

Study `apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/index.tsx` for patterns (formatDate, getInitials, how it renders athlete rows) and lift utilities into local helpers in the new file — don't extract shared modules yet.

## Page structure (vertical, Card-based)

1. **Page header**
   - Back button → `/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes` (TanStack `Link` with `to: ".."` or explicit path)
   - H1: captain full name, or team name if team division (fall back to captain email)
   - Subtitle: division label + status badge (active / removed / invite-pending etc.)
   - Action buttons on the right:
     - "Change Division" → opens existing `<TransferDivisionDialog />` from `../-components/transfer-division-dialog`
     - "Remove Registration" → red destructive button with confirm `AlertDialog` calling `removeRegistrationFn`; on success navigate back to list

2. **Registration Info Card** (grid of inline-editable fields)
   - Team Name (only for team divisions): inline editable text input with Save/Cancel buttons; commits via `updateRegistrationTeamNameFn`
   - Division: shows current; button "Change" opens TransferDivisionDialog
   - Affiliate: read-only (from metadata / user.affiliateName) — show muted
   - Registered at: read-only date
   - Payment status: read-only badge + paid amount if `commercePurchase`
   - Registration status: read-only

3. **Captain / Athlete Basic Info** (editable)
   - A small card-per-member section titled by role (Captain / Teammate)
   - Fields per member: First name, Last name, Email (all text inputs), edit mode toggle, save/cancel
   - Save → `updateAthleteUserProfileFn` with `userId` of that member
   - **Warning banner** if member is a real user (not placeholder — check if emailVerified/hasPassword; the detail fn doesn't return that, so just show a generic muted note: "Editing the name/email updates this user's account profile.")

4. **Team Members section** (only render for team divisions i.e. `detail.division?.teamSize > 1`)
   - Heading "Team Members (N / maxSize)" where maxSize = division.teamSize, N = active memberships + accepted + pending invites
   - Current active members: list each with avatar, name, email, "(captain)" badge. Each has a destructive "Remove" button that confirms, then calls `removeTeammateFromRegistrationFn` (disabled for the captain)
   - Pending invites: list each pending invite with email, guestName (if accepted-and-awaiting-account), created date, expires date. Actions: "Resend" (calls `refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn`), "Cancel Invite" (calls `cancelPendingTeamInviteFn`)
   - "Add Teammate" button — only shown if current member count < teamSize. Opens a dialog with fields: email (required), first name, last name. Submit calls `addTeammateToRegistrationFn`. Client-side: prevent duplicate emails.

5. **Registration Answers Card**
   - For each question in `detail.questions`, for each member of the registration: render an editable row with question label, member name (if team), and an inline input/textarea for the answer.
   - Save on blur or with a save button. Commits via `updateRegistrationAnswerFn` (userId + questionId + answer).
   - If no questions, don't render the card.

6. **Waivers Card** (read-only for now)
   - List each waiver with a per-member column showing "Signed MMM DD" (green) or "Not signed" (muted). Don't build editing yet.

7. **Scores / Submissions Section**
   - Render ONE of:
     - If `competition.competitionType === "online"`: **Video Submissions** section — one sub-card per event in `detail.events`:
       - Event title (workoutName), submission window dates
       - Existing submissions for this event (filtered from `detail.videoSubmissions` by `trackWorkoutId`): for each:
         - Show `<VideoPlayerEmbed url={submission.videoUrl} />` inline (or a smaller thumbnail-style render with link)
         - Show current score + scoreStatus from `detail.scores` (matched by trackWorkoutId + submission.userId)
         - Actions: "Edit Video URL" (uses `<OrganizerVideoLinksEditor />` pre-filled with the existing submissions for the event for this registration), "Edit Score" (inline `<EnterScoreForm />`), "Delete" (calls `deleteOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`)
       - If no submission yet for this event: show an empty state with an "Add Submission Manually" button that opens a dialog with fields: member (if team, select which userId from athlete team), videoUrl, videoIndex (auto), score (uses EnterScoreForm-style inputs — pick the minimal set: score, scoreStatus, optional secondaryScore/tieBreakScore), notes. Submit calls `createOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`.
       - If partial team submission (e.g. captain submitted but teammate slot empty): show each expected slot (0..teamSize-1) with either its submission or an "Add" affordance.
     - If `competition.competitionType === "in-person"`: **Scores** section — one sub-card per event:
       - Event title, workout scheme
       - Existing score for captain (most common) and any other members: show score, scoreStatus, rounds
       - Actions: "Edit Score" (inline `<EnterScoreForm />` wired to `saveCompetitionScoreFn`), "Delete Score" (calls `deleteCompetitionScoreFn`)
       - If no score: show "Enter Score" button opening the same form.

## Quality bar

- Follow the `/frontend-design` skill guidance — don't just throw shadcn cards on the page; use generous spacing (py-8, space-y-6), clear section headers with Description text, muted labels, good empty states, inline editing with optimistic UI where easy (otherwise `router.invalidate()` after mutations).
- Every mutation: toast success + error via `sonner`, invalidate the route.
- Loading states for inline edits.
- Accessibility: proper labels, aria, focus handling in dialogs.
- Mobile: stack gracefully. Don't need table; cards work.
- Respect existing color conventions (destructive for red actions, muted-foreground for secondary text, green-600 for success indicators).

## Also update the list page `athletes/index.tsx`

Make each athlete row link to the new detail page. Two changes:
1. Wrap the athlete's displayed name (desktop table cell AND mobile card heading) in a `<Link to="/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId" params={{ competitionId: competition.id, registrationId: row.registrationId }}>`. Style with `hover:underline` and `font-medium` (no visible color change).
2. Add a new last column (label "View") **before** the existing actions column on desktop; cell renders an icon-only `<Link>` button with `ArrowUpRight` (lucide icon) and aria-label="View athlete details". On mobile card, add a matching "View details →" link at the bottom of the card body.
3. Also the row for a NON-captain teammate (row.isCaptain === false) should also link to the same detail page (same registrationId) — the detail page handles all members.
4. Do NOT remove any existing dropdown menu actions. Keep Transfer / Change Division / Remove where they are.

## Constraints

- Do not change any server function.
- Do not change any existing component in `src/components/compete/` — reuse them.
- Keep total file size manageable — break inline components (TeamMembersSection, AnswersSection, VideoSubmissionsSection, ScoresSection, ManualSubmissionDialog, AddTeammateDialog, etc.) into co-located components under `apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/-components/*.tsx`. Each sub-component owns its own file ≤ 250 lines.
- Add `// @lat: [[organizer-dashboard#Registrations (Athletes)]]` at the top of the new detail route file.
- Add an `import "server-only"` guard at the top is NOT needed because route files are dual (server + client). Follow existing conventions.

## Validation before finishing

Run from repo root:
```
pnpm --filter wodsmith-start type-check
pnpm --filter wodsmith-start lint
```

If you don't have a competition ID handy, navigate to the organizer dashboard and pick one; if auth blocks you, skip this step and just ensure type-check + lint pass.

toolUseResult.prompt:
You're building the main new UI for the WODsmith "Organizer Athlete Detail" feature. This page lets an organizer edit **every** field of a single athlete's registration plus all their scores/video submissions.

**Before coding, invoke the `/frontend-design` skill** via the Skill tool to load the design guidance, then follow it. The guidance governs layout, typography, whitespace, empty states, affordances, etc.

# Context
- Repo: `/Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/apps/wodsmith-start/`
- Tech: TanStack Start (React 19, file-based routing), Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui (in `src/components/ui/`), sonner for toasts, TanStack Router `Link` / `useNavigate` / `useRouter`.
- Server fns available at `@/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns` (just built):
  - `getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn` — fetches the full picture for the page (registration, captain, athleteTeam+memberships, pendingInvites, questions, answers, waivers, waiverSignatures, events, videoSubmissions, scores, division, competition, commercePurchase)
  - `updateRegistrationTeamNameFn`
  - `updateAthleteUserProfileFn` (firstName/lastName/email — edits the user row)
  - `updateRegistrationAnswerFn`
  - `removeTeammateFromRegistrationFn`
  - `cancelPendingTeamInviteFn`
  - `addTeammateToRegistrationFn`
  - `createOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`
  - `deleteOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`
- Existing server fns to also use:
  - `transferRegistrationDivisionFn` from `@/server-fns/registration-fns` (change division)
  - `removeRegistrationFn` from `@/server-fns/registration-fns`
  - `refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn` from `@/server-fns/registration-fns` (resend invite)
  - `updateSubmissionVideoUrlFn` from `@/server-fns/video-submission-fns` (edit video URL on existing submission)
  - `verifySubmissionScoreFn` / `enterSubmissionScoreFn` from `@/server-fns/submission-verification-fns` (edit/enter score)
  - `saveCompetitionScoreFn` from `@/server-fns/competition-score-fns` (in-person scoring)
  - `deleteCompetitionScoreFn` from `@/server-fns/competition-score-fns`
  - `getCompetitionDivisionsWithCountsFn` from `@/server-fns/competition-divisions-fns`
- Reusable components:
  - `OrganizerVideoLinksEditor` at `@/components/compete/organizer-video-links-editor`
  - `EnterScoreForm` at `@/components/compete/enter-score-form`
  - `VideoPlayerEmbed` at `@/components/compete/video-player-embed`
  - Sidebar/breadcrumb: parent route `$competitionId.tsx` wraps children with `CompetitionSidebar` and `OrganizerBreadcrumb`. Don't re-add those.

# File to create

`apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId.tsx`

(The sibling `athletes/index.tsx` already exists — that's the list page.)

## Route definition pattern

```ts
import { createFileRoute, getRouteApi, Link, useNavigate, useRouter } from "@tanstack/react-router"
import { useServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
// ...

const parentRoute = getRouteApi("/compete/organizer/$competitionId")

export const Route = createFileRoute("/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId")({
  staleTime: 10_000,
  component: AthleteDetailPage,
  loader: async ({ params, parentMatchPromise }) => {
    const parentMatch = await parentMatchPromise
    const { competition } = parentMatch.loaderData!
    const [detail, divisionsResult] = await Promise.all([
      getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn({ data: { registrationId: params.registrationId, competitionId: params.competitionId } }),
      getCompetitionDivisionsWithCountsFn({ data: { competitionId: params.competitionId, teamId: competition.organizingTeamId } }),
    ])
    return { detail, divisions: divisionsResult.divisions }
  },
})
```

Study `apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/index.tsx` for patterns (formatDate, getInitials, how it renders athlete rows) and lift utilities into local helpers in the new file — don't extract shared modules yet.

## Page structure (vertical, Card-based)

1. **Page header**
   - Back button → `/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes` (TanStack `Link` with `to: ".."` or explicit path)
   - H1: captain full name, or team name if team division (fall back to captain email)
   - Subtitle: division label + status badge (active / removed / invite-pending etc.)
   - Action buttons on the right:
     - "Change Division" → opens existing `<TransferDivisionDialog />` from `../-components/transfer-division-dialog`
     - "Remove Registration" → red destructive button with confirm `AlertDialog` calling `removeRegistrationFn`; on success navigate back to list

2. **Registration Info Card** (grid of inline-editable fields)
   - Team Name (only for team divisions): inline editable text input with Save/Cancel buttons; commits via `updateRegistrationTeamNameFn`
   - Division: shows current; button "Change" opens TransferDivisionDialog
   - Affiliate: read-only (from metadata / user.affiliateName) — show muted
   - Registered at: read-only date
   - Payment status: read-only badge + paid amount if `commercePurchase`
   - Registration status: read-only

3. **Captain / Athlete Basic Info** (editable)
   - A small card-per-member section titled by role (Captain / Teammate)
   - Fields per member: First name, Last name, Email (all text inputs), edit mode toggle, save/cancel
   - Save → `updateAthleteUserProfileFn` with `userId` of that member
   - **Warning banner** if member is a real user (not placeholder — check if emailVerified/hasPassword; the detail fn doesn't return that, so just show a generic muted note: "Editing the name/email updates this user's account profile.")

4. **Team Members section** (only render for team divisions i.e. `detail.division?.teamSize > 1`)
   - Heading "Team Members (N / maxSize)" where maxSize = division.teamSize, N = active memberships + accepted + pending invites
   - Current active members: list each with avatar, name, email, "(captain)" badge. Each has a destructive "Remove" button that confirms, then calls `removeTeammateFromRegistrationFn` (disabled for the captain)
   - Pending invites: list each pending invite with email, guestName (if accepted-and-awaiting-account), created date, expires date. Actions: "Resend" (calls `refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn`), "Cancel Invite" (calls `cancelPendingTeamInviteFn`)
   - "Add Teammate" button — only shown if current member count < teamSize. Opens a dialog with fields: email (required), first name, last name. Submit calls `addTeammateToRegistrationFn`. Client-side: prevent duplicate emails.

5. **Registration Answers Card**
   - For each question in `detail.questions`, for each member of the registration: render an editable row with question label, member name (if team), and an inline input/textarea for the answer.
   - Save on blur or with a save button. Commits via `updateRegistrationAnswerFn` (userId + questionId + answer).
   - If no questions, don't render the card.

6. **Waivers Card** (read-only for now)
   - List each waiver with a per-member column showing "Signed MMM DD" (green) or "Not signed" (muted). Don't build editing yet.

7. **Scores / Submissions Section**
   - Render ONE of:
     - If `competition.competitionType === "online"`: **Video Submissions** section — one sub-card per event in `detail.events`:
       - Event title (workoutName), submission window dates
       - Existing submissions for this event (filtered from `detail.videoSubmissions` by `trackWorkoutId`): for each:
         - Show `<VideoPlayerEmbed url={submission.videoUrl} />` inline (or a smaller thumbnail-style render with link)
         - Show current score + scoreStatus from `detail.scores` (matched by trackWorkoutId + submission.userId)
         - Actions: "Edit Video URL" (uses `<OrganizerVideoLinksEditor />` pre-filled with the existing submissions for the event for this registration), "Edit Score" (inline `<EnterScoreForm />`), "Delete" (calls `deleteOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`)
       - If no submission yet for this event: show an empty state with an "Add Submission Manually" button that opens a dialog with fields: member (if team, select which userId from athlete team), videoUrl, videoIndex (auto), score (uses EnterScoreForm-style inputs — pick the minimal set: score, scoreStatus, optional secondaryScore/tieBreakScore), notes. Submit calls `createOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn`.
       - If partial team submission (e.g. captain submitted but teammate slot empty): show each expected slot (0..teamSize-1) with either its submission or an "Add" affordance.
     - If `competition.competitionType === "in-person"`: **Scores** section — one sub-card per event:
       - Event title, workout scheme
       - Existing score for captain (most common) and any other members: show score, scoreStatus, rounds
       - Actions: "Edit Score" (inline `<EnterScoreForm />` wired to `saveCompetitionScoreFn`), "Delete Score" (calls `deleteCompetitionScoreFn`)
       - If no score: show "Enter Score" button opening the same form.

## Quality bar

- Follow the `/frontend-design` skill guidance — don't just throw shadcn cards on the page; use generous spacing (py-8, space-y-6), clear section headers with Description text, muted labels, good empty states, inline editing with optimistic UI where easy (otherwise `router.invalidate()` after mutations).
- Every mutation: toast success + error via `sonner`, invalidate the route.
- Loading states for inline edits.
- Accessibility: proper labels, aria, focus handling in dialogs.
- Mobile: stack gracefully. Don't need table; cards work.
- Respect existing color conventions (destructive for red actions, muted-foreground for secondary text, green-600 for success indicators).

## Also update the list page `athletes/index.tsx`

Make each athlete row link to the new detail page. Two changes:
1. Wrap the athlete's displayed name (desktop table cell AND mobile card heading) in a `<Link to="/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId" params={{ competitionId: competition.id, registrationId: row.registrationId }}>`. Style with `hover:underline` and `font-medium` (no visible color change).
2. Add a new last column (label "View") **before** the existing actions column on desktop; cell renders an icon-only `<Link>` button with `ArrowUpRight` (lucide icon) and aria-label="View athlete details". On mobile card, add a matching "View details →" link at the bottom of the card body.
3. Also the row for a NON-captain teammate (row.isCaptain === false) should also link to the same detail page (same registrationId) — the detail page handles all members.
4. Do NOT remove any existing dropdown menu actions. Keep Transfer / Change Division / Remove where they are.

## Constraints

- Do not change any server function.
- Do not change any existing component in `src/components/compete/` — reuse them.
- Keep total file size manageable — break inline components (TeamMembersSection, AnswersSection, VideoSubmissionsSection, ScoresSection, ManualSubmissionDialog, AddTeammateDialog, etc.) into co-located components under `apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/-components/*.tsx`. Each sub-component owns its own file ≤ 250 lines.
- Add `// @lat: [[organizer-dashboard#Registrations (Athletes)]]` at the top of the new detail route file.
- Add an `import "server-only"` guard at the top is NOT needed because route files are dual (server + client). Follow existing conventions.

## Validation before finishing

Run from repo root:
```
pnpm --filter wodsmith-start type-check
pnpm --filter wodsmith-start lint
```
Fix all errors. Existing lint warnings are fine.

Also: start the dev server with `pnpm --filter wodsmith-start dev` in the background, wait for it to come up, then use the playwright MCP to navigate to `http://localhost:3000/compete/organizer/<any-competition-id>/athletes` and click through to a detail page to verify it loads without a red screen. If you don't have a competition ID handy, navigate to the organizer dashboard and pick one; if auth blocks you, skip this step and just ensure type-check + lint pass.

# Report when done
- New file paths (detail route + sub-components).
- How many lines.
- Any trimmed/deferred features (if any).
- Type-check + lint status.
- Any screenshot captured (path).
Under 500 words.

2074	export const refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn = createServerFn({
2075	  method: "POST",
2076	})
2077	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) => refreshInviteSchema.parse(data))
2078	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
2079	    const session = await requireVerifiedEmail()
2080	    const db = getDb()
2081	
2082	    // Verify the registration exists and the caller is the captain
2083	    const registration =
2084	      await db.query.competitionRegistrationsTable.findFirst({
2085	        where: eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.id, data.registrationId),
2086	        with: {
2087	          competition: {
2088	            columns: { id: true, name: true, slug: true },
2089	          },
2090	          division: {
2091	            columns: { id: true, label: true },
2092	          },
2093	        },
2094	      })
2095	
2096	    if (!registration) {
2097	      throw new Error("Registration not found")
2098	    }
2099	
2100	    if (registration.userId !== session.userId) {
2101	      throw new Error("Only the team captain can refresh invites")
2102	    }
2103	
2104	    // Get the invitation (only pending invites can be refreshed)
2105	    const { INVITATION_STATUS } = await import("@/db/schema")
2106	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
2107	      where: and(
2108	        eq(teamInvitationTable.id, data.invitationId),
2109	        eq(
2110	          teamInvitationTable.teamId,
2111	          registration.athleteTeamId ?? "",
2112	        ),
2113	        eq(teamInvitationTable.status, INVITATION_STATUS.PENDING),
2114	      ),
2115	    })
2116	
2117	    if (!invitation) {
2118	      throw new Error("Invitation not found")
2119	    }
2120	
2121	    if (invitation.acceptedAt) {
2122	      throw new Error("Invitation has already been accepted")
2123	    }
2124	
2125	    // Only allow refreshing expired invitations
2126	    if (!invitation.expiresAt || new Date(invitation.expiresAt) >= new Date()) {
2127	      throw new Error("Only expired invitations can be refreshed")
2128	    }
2129	
2130	    // Generate new token and extend expiry to 30 days
2131	    const newToken = createId()
2132	    const newExpiresAt = new Date()
2133	    newExpiresAt.setDate(newExpiresAt.getDate() + 30)
2134	
2135	    await db
2136	      .update(teamInvitationTable)
2137	      .set({
2138	        token: newToken,
2139	        expiresAt: newExpiresAt,
2140	        updatedAt: new Date(),
2141	      })
2142	      .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, invitation.id))
2143	
2144	    // Resend the invite email
2145	    const competition = Array.isArray(registration.competition)
2146	      ? registration.competition[0]
2147	      : registration.competition
2148	    const division = Array.isArray(registration.division)
2149	      ? registration.division[0]
2150	      : registration.division
2151	
2152	    const inviter = await db.query.userTable.findFirst({
2153	      where: eq(userTable.id, session.userId),
2154	      columns: { firstName: true, lastName: true },
2155	    })
2156	    const inviterName = inviter
2157	      ? `${inviter.firstName || ""} ${inviter.lastName || ""}`.trim() ||
2158	        "Your team captain"
2159	      : "Your team captain"
2160	
2161	    const { sendCompetitionTeamInviteEmail } = await import("@/utils/email")
2162	    await sendCompetitionTeamInviteEmail({
2163	      email: invitation.email,
2164	      invitationToken: newToken,
2165	      teamName: registration.teamName ?? "Team",
2166	      competitionName: competition?.name ?? "the competition",
2167	      divisionName: division?.label ?? "Division",
2168	      inviterName,
2169	    })
2170	
2171	    return { success: true, newToken }
2172	  })

864	export const cancelPendingTeamInviteFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
865	  .inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
866	    cancelPendingTeamInviteInputSchema.parse(data),
867	  )
868	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
869	    const session = await requireVerifiedEmail()
870	    if (!session) throw new Error("Unauthorized")
871	
872	    const db = getDb()
873	    updateRequestContext({ userId: session.userId })
874	    addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", data.competitionId)
875	    addRequestContextAttribute("registrationId", data.registrationId)
876	    addRequestContextAttribute("invitationId", data.invitationId)
877	
878	    const competition = await loadCompetitionForAuth(data.competitionId)
879	    requireCanManage(session, competition.organizingTeamId)
880	
881	    const registration = await loadRegistrationOrThrow(
882	      data.registrationId,
883	      data.competitionId,
884	    )
885	
886	    if (!registration.athleteTeamId) {
887	      throw new Error("This registration has no athlete team")
888	    }
889	
890	    // Make sure invite belongs to this registration's athlete team
891	    const invitation = await db.query.teamInvitationTable.findFirst({
892	      where: and(
893	        eq(teamInvitationTable.id, data.invitationId),
894	        eq(teamInvitationTable.teamId, registration.athleteTeamId),
895	      ),
896	    })
897	    if (!invitation) throw new Error("Invitation not found")
898	
899	    await db
900	      .update(teamInvitationTable)
901	      .set({
902	        status: INVITATION_STATUS.CANCELLED,
903	        updatedAt: new Date(),
904	      })
905	      .where(eq(teamInvitationTable.id, data.invitationId))
906	
907	    logEntityUpdated({
908	      entity: "team_invitation",
909	      id: data.invitationId,
910	      fields: ["status"],
911	      attributes: {
912	        competitionId: data.competitionId,
913	        registrationId: data.registrationId,
914	        newStatus: INVITATION_STATUS.CANCELLED,
915	      },
916	    })
917	
918	    return { success: true }
919	  })
920	
921	// ============================================================================
922	// A7. addTeammateToRegistrationFn
923	// ============================================================================
924	
925	const addTeammateToRegistrationInputSchema = z.object({
926	  registrationId: z.string().min(1),
927	  competitionId: z.string().min(1),
928	  email: z.string().email().max(255),
929	  firstName: z.string().max(255).optional(),
930	  lastName: z.string().max(255).optional(),
931	})
932	
933	export const addTeammateToRegistrationFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })

81	function requireCanManage(

83	  organizingTeamId: string,

86	    session.user?.role === ROLES_ENUM.ADMIN ||
87	    !!session.teams?.find(
88	      (t) =>
89	        t.id === organizingTeamId &&
90	        t.permissions.includes(TEAM_PERMISSIONS.MANAGE_COMPETITIONS),
91	    )
92	  if (!canManage) throw new Error("Forbidden")
93	}

102	      eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.id, registrationId),
103	      eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId, competitionId),
104	    ),
105	  })
106	  if (!registration) throw new Error("Registration not found")
107	  return registration
108	}

1297	  )
1298	  .handler(async ({ data }) => {
1299	    const session = await requireVerifiedEmail()
1300	    if (!session) throw new Error("Unauthorized")
1301	
1302	    const db = getDb()
1303	    updateRequestContext({ userId: session.userId })
1304	    addRequestContextAttribute("competitionId", data.competitionId)
1305	    addRequestContextAttribute("submissionId", data.submissionId)
1306	
1307	    const competition = await loadCompetitionForAuth(data.competitionId)
1308	    requireCanManage(session, competition.organizingTeamId)
1309	
1310	    // Load submission + verify it belongs to a registration in this competition
1311	    const submission = await db.query.videoSubmissionsTable.findFirst({
1312	      where: eq(videoSubmissionsTable.id, data.submissionId),
1313	    })
1314	    if (!submission) throw new Error("Video submission not found")
1315	
1316	    const registration = await db.query.competitionRegistrationsTable.findFirst(
1317	      {
1318	        where: and(
1319	          eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.id, submission.registrationId),
1320	          eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId, data.competitionId),
1321	        ),
1322	      },
1323	    )
1324	    if (!registration)
1325	      throw new Error("Submission does not belong to this competition")
1326	
1327	    // If videoIndex === 0 this is the score owner — delete the linked score too
1328	    if (submission.videoIndex === 0) {
1329	      // Find the score keyed by (competitionEventId = trackWorkoutId, userId)
1330	      const [score] = await db
1331	        .select({ id: scoresTable.id })
1332	        .from(scoresTable)
1333	        .where(
1334	          and(
1335	            eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, submission.trackWorkoutId),
1336	            eq(scoresTable.userId, submission.userId),
1337	          ),
1338	        )
1339	        .limit(1)
1340	      if (score) {
1341	        await db
1342	          .delete(scoreRoundsTable)
1343	          .where(eq(scoreRoundsTable.scoreId, score.id))
1344	        await db.delete(scoresTable).where(eq(scoresTable.id, score.id))
1345	        logEntityDeleted({
1346	          entity: "score",
1347	          id: score.id,
1348	          attributes: {
1349	            competitionId: data.competitionId,
1350	            trackWorkoutId: submission.trackWorkoutId,
1351	            userId: submission.userId,
1352	          },
1353	        })
1354	      }
1355	    }
1356	
1357	    await db
1358	      .delete(videoSubmissionsTable)
1359	      .where(eq(videoSubmissionsTable.id, data.submissionId))
1360	
1361	    logEntityDeleted({
1362	      entity: "video_submission",
1363	      id: data.submissionId,
1364	      attributes: {
1365	        competitionId: data.competitionId,
1366	        registrationId: submission.registrationId,
1367	        trackWorkoutId: submission.trackWorkoutId,
1368	      },
1369	    })
1370	
1371	    return { success: true }

content.content:
1	# Organizer Dashboard
2	
3	The competition organizer dashboard at `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}` is the central hub for managing all aspects of a competition.
4	
5	## Layout and Access Control
6	
7	The layout route fetches competition data and verifies the user has organizer-level access before rendering the sidebar and child routes.
8	
9	Access requires authentication plus one of: platform admin role, or owner/admin membership on the competition's `organizingTeamId`. The layout provides competition data to all child routes via `parentMatchPromise`, avoiding redundant fetches. Each child route uses `getRouteApi("/compete/organizer/$competitionId")` to access parent loader data.
10	
11	## Overview Page
12	
13	The index page shows at-a-glance competition stats and quick action cards for common organizer tasks.
14	
15	Parallel-fetches registrations, revenue stats, events, heats, division results status, and submission windows (online-only). Displays quick-action components for events, heats, submission windows, and division results that guide organizers through setup steps.
16	
17	## Competition Editing
18	
19	The edit page allows organizers to modify competition name, dates, description, registration window, timezone, and series group.
20	
21	Fetches competition groups for the organizing team. Uses `OrganizerCompetitionEditForm` component.
22	
23	## Division Management
24	
25	Organizers configure which divisions athletes can register into, sourced from the team's scaling groups.
26	
27	Fetches divisions with registration counts, scaling groups, and series mapping status in parallel. Uses `OrganizerDivisionManager` for CRUD on divisions. Also includes `CapacitySettingsForm` for per-division and default max spots configuration. For series competitions, shows mapping status indicating whether divisions are synced from the series template.
28	
29	## Event Management
30	
31	Events link workouts to the competition. Each event represents a workout athletes will perform and be scored on.
32	
33	Fetches events, divisions, movements, and sponsors in parallel. Uses `OrganizerEventManager` for creating, editing, reordering, and deleting events. Events can have per-division workout descriptions, attached resources, and judging sheets. Supports parent/sub-event hierarchy for multi-workout events. Publishing or unpublishing a parent event cascades to all its child sub-events via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/-components/quick-actions-events.tsx]].
34	
35	### Event-Division Mappings
36	
37	Controls which events are visible to which divisions within a competition via `event_division_mappings` ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/event-division-mappings.ts#eventDivisionMappingsTable]]).
38	
39	If NO mappings exist for a competition, all events apply to all divisions (backwards compatible). If mappings exist for an event, only the mapped divisions see and score that event. An event with no explicit mappings remains visible to all divisions even when other events in the competition have mappings (partial mapping). An event can map to multiple divisions; a division can have multiple events. Cascade cleanup is handled at the application level since PlanetScale does not enforce foreign keys. Division IDs in mappings come from `competition_divisions.divisionId` (the same IDs registrations use), not directly from `scaling_levels` — this ensures the leaderboard's division filtering matches.
40	
41	The save endpoint ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/event-division-mapping-fns.ts#saveEventDivisionMappingsFn]]) validates all submitted trackWorkoutId/divisionId pairs belong to the competition before inserting, since PlanetScale has no FK enforcement. The read endpoint filters returned mappings against current events and divisions, discarding stale rows from changed programming tracks or scaling groups. DB error handling only catches `ER_NO_SUCH_TABLE` (for fresh deployments); all other errors propagate.
42	
43	Sub-events inherit their parent container event's division mappings — only top-level events are mapped in the matrix. The workouts list ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/workouts/index.tsx]]) also filters child events by division mappings, preventing sub-events from leaking across divisions. The workout count badge reflects the filtered count. The leaderboard ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts]]) and event detail page ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/workouts/$eventId.tsx]]) both check the parent's mappings when evaluating a sub-event's visibility. The event detail page falls back to showing all divisions when the specific event has no mappings. The leaderboard UI ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/leaderboard-page-content.tsx]]) validates `selectedEventId` against the division-filtered events list via `effectiveEventId`, so stale URL params (e.g. shared links across divisions) fall back to the overall view.
44	
45	The event edit form ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/events/event-details-form.tsx]]) also respects mappings — the "Division Variations" section only shows divisions mapped to the current event. If no mappings exist for the competition, all divisions are shown (backwards compatible). The athlete score submission panel ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/athlete-score-submission-panel.tsx#AthleteScoreSubmissionPanel]]) on the competition overview page also filters workouts by division mappings, so athletes only see events mapped to their selected division.
46	
47	## Heat Scheduling
48	
49	The schedule page manages heats — time blocks where groups of athletes perform a workout together at a venue.
50	
51	Fetches venues, events, heats, divisions, and registrations in parallel. Uses `SchedulePageClient` which contains `VenueManager` and `HeatScheduleManager` components. Heats have start times, lane counts, division/venue assignments, and athlete assignments. Only available for in-person competitions.
52	
53	## Locations (Venues)
54	
55	CRUD management of physical venues where competition events take place.
56	
57	Each venue has a name, address, and capacity. Venues are referenced by heats for scheduling.
58	
59	## Registrations (Athletes)
60	
61	Lists all registered athletes with division, payment status, team info, and management actions.
62	
63	Uses `getOrganizerRegistrationsFn` for the full registration list with detailed athlete info. Features include:
64	- Sortable table with division and status filters
65	- Manual registration dialog (organizer adds athletes directly — see [[registration#Registration Flow#Organizer Manual Registration]])
66	- Remove registration (soft-delete with cascading cleanup — see [[registration#Registration Removal]])
67	- Transfer registration to a different division (see [[registration#Division Transfer]])
68	- Transfer registration to a different person (purchase transfer)
69	- CSV export of athlete data
70	- Registration questions editor (custom questions athletes answer during signup)
71	- Pending teammate invitations tab for team divisions
72	
73	## Scoring Configuration
74	
75	Configures the algorithm used to rank athletes on the leaderboard.
76	
77	Fetches events for head-to-head tiebreaker selection. Uses `ScoringSettingsForm` component. Settings include scoring algorithm, point distribution, and tiebreak rules.
78	
79	## Results Entry
80	
81	Organizers enter scores for each athlete per event, or review video submissions for online competitions.
82	
83	For **in-person competitions**: `ResultsEntryForm` provides a per-event, per-division score entry grid. Organizers select an event and division, then enter scores for each athlete. Supports publishing/unpublishing division results. Multi-round scores flow through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], which for `time-with-cap` workouts derives per-round cap status server-side from each round's encoded value against `workout.timeCap * 1000`, persists that status on `scoreRoundsTable`, preserves the summed total on `scoresTable.scoreValue` (instead of clamping to the cap), and threads `cappedRoundCount` into `computeSortKey` so the leaderboard tiebreaker honors "fewer capped rounds wins".
84	
85	For **online competitions**: Shows a submissions overview with links to individual video verification pages at `/events/{eventId}/submissions/`. Each child sub-event is listed as its own row with submission counts and verification status; the parent event name is shown as contextual metadata alongside each child row. Events without `competition_events` rows (no submission window configured) are handled gracefully.
86	
87	### Division Results Publish Gate
88	
89	Controls whether scores for a given (event, division) pair are visible on the public leaderboard. Organizers toggle publish state per event-division from the results entry UI.
90	
91	Publish state lives in `competitionsTable.settings.divisionResults[trackWorkoutId][divisionId].publishedAt` — an ISO timestamp when published, absent/null when in draft. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] reads this shape and gates two flows with it:
92	
93	1. Per-event leaderboard entries: inside the per-event loop, `if (!divisionPublishState?.publishedAt) continue` drops any (event, division) that isn't published.
94	2. Video visibility: the `isEventDivisionPublished` helper returns `false` for unpublished pairs so their videos don't leak.
95	
96	Defaults: when `divisionResults` is absent entirely, online competitions treat everything as hidden (opt-in publishing) while in-person competitions show everything (backwards compat for gyms that never opted into the gate). Organizers can bulk-publish all divisions for an event from `QuickActionsDivisionResults`.
97	
98	## Submission Windows
99	
100	Manages time windows during which athletes can submit video evidence for online competitions.

file.content:
# Organizer Dashboard

The competition organizer dashboard at `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}` is the central hub for managing all aspects of a competition.

## Layout and Access Control

The layout route fetches competition data and verifies the user has organizer-level access before rendering the sidebar and child routes.

Access requires authentication plus one of: platform admin role, or owner/admin membership on the competition's `organizingTeamId`. The layout provides competition data to all child routes via `parentMatchPromise`, avoiding redundant fetches. Each child route uses `getRouteApi("/compete/organizer/$competitionId")` to access parent loader data.

## Overview Page

The index page shows at-a-glance competition stats and quick action cards for common organizer tasks.

Parallel-fetches registrations, revenue stats, events, heats, division results status, and submission windows (online-only). Displays quick-action components for events, heats, submission windows, and division results that guide organizers through setup steps.

## Competition Editing

The edit page allows organizers to modify competition name, dates, description, registration window, timezone, and series group.

Fetches competition groups for the organizing team. Uses `OrganizerCompetitionEditForm` component.

## Division Management

Organizers configure which divisions athletes can register into, sourced from the team's scaling groups.

Fetches divisions with registration counts, scaling groups, and series mapping status in parallel. Uses `OrganizerDivisionManager` for CRUD on divisions. Also includes `CapacitySettingsForm` for per-division and default max spots configuration. For series competitions, shows mapping status indicating whether divisions are synced from the series template.

## Event Management

Events link workouts to the competition. Each event represents a workout athletes will perform and be scored on.

Fetches events, divisions, movements, and sponsors in parallel. Uses `OrganizerEventManager` for creating, editing, reordering, and deleting events. Events can have per-division workout descriptions, attached resources, and judging sheets. Supports parent/sub-event hierarchy for multi-workout events. Publishing or unpublishing a parent event cascades to all its child sub-events via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/-components/quick-actions-events.tsx]].

### Event-Division Mappings

Controls which events are visible to which divisions within a competition via `event_division_mappings` ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/db/schemas/event-division-mappings.ts#eventDivisionMappingsTable]]).

If NO mappings exist for a competition, all events apply to all divisions (backwards compatible). If mappings exist for an event, only the mapped divisions see and score that event. An event with no explicit mappings remains visible to all divisions even when other events in the competition have mappings (partial mapping). An event can map to multiple divisions; a division can have multiple events. Cascade cleanup is handled at the application level since PlanetScale does not enforce foreign keys. Division IDs in mappings come from `competition_divisions.divisionId` (the same IDs registrations use), not directly from `scaling_levels` — this ensures the leaderboard's division filtering matches.

The save endpoint ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/event-division-mapping-fns.ts#saveEventDivisionMappingsFn]]) validates all submitted trackWorkoutId/divisionId pairs belong to the competition before inserting, since PlanetScale has no FK enforcement. The read endpoint filters returned mappings against current events and divisions, discarding stale rows from changed programming tracks or scaling groups. DB error handling only catches `ER_NO_SUCH_TABLE` (for fresh deployments); all other errors propagate.

Sub-events inherit their parent container event's division mappings — only top-level events are mapped in the matrix. The workouts list ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/workouts/index.tsx]]) also filters child events by division mappings, preventing sub-events from leaking across divisions. The workout count badge reflects the filtered count. The leaderboard ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts]]) and event detail page ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/workouts/$eventId.tsx]]) both check the parent's mappings when evaluating a sub-event's visibility. The event detail page falls back to showing all divisions when the specific event has no mappings. The leaderboard UI ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/leaderboard-page-content.tsx]]) validates `selectedEventId` against the division-filtered events list via `effectiveEventId`, so stale URL params (e.g. shared links across divisions) fall back to the overall view.

The event edit form ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/events/event-details-form.tsx]]) also respects mappings — the "Division Variations" section only shows divisions mapped to the current event. If no mappings exist for the competition, all divisions are shown (backwards compatible). The athlete score submission panel ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/athlete-score-submission-panel.tsx#AthleteScoreSubmissionPanel]]) on the competition overview page also filters workouts by division mappings, so athletes only see events mapped to their selected division.

## Heat Scheduling

The schedule page manages heats — time blocks where groups of athletes perform a workout together at a venue.

Fetches venues, events, heats, divisions, and registrations in parallel. Uses `SchedulePageClient` which contains `VenueManager` and `HeatScheduleManager` components. Heats have start times, lane counts, division/venue assignments, and athlete assignments. Only available for in-person competitions.

## Locations (Venues)

CRUD management of physical venues where competition events take place.

Each venue has a name, address, and capacity. Venues are referenced by heats for scheduling.

## Registrations (Athletes)

Lists all registered athletes with division, payment status, team info, and management actions.

Uses `getOrganizerRegistrationsFn` for the full registration list with detailed athlete info. Features include:
- Sortable table with division and status filters
- Manual registration dialog (organizer adds athletes directly — see [[registration#Registration Flow#Organizer Manual Registration]])
- Remove registration (soft-delete with cascading cleanup — see [[registration#Registration Removal]])
- Transfer registration to a different division (see [[registration#Division Transfer]])
- Transfer registration to a different person (purchase transfer)
- CSV export of athlete data
- Registration questions editor (custom questions athletes answer during signup)
- Pending teammate invitations tab for team divisions

## Scoring Configuration

Configures the algorithm used to rank athletes on the leaderboard.

Fetches events for head-to-head tiebreaker selection. Uses `ScoringSettingsForm` component. Settings include scoring algorithm, point distribution, and tiebreak rules.

## Results Entry

Organizers enter scores for each athlete per event, or review video submissions for online competitions.

For **in-person competitions**: `ResultsEntryForm` provides a per-event, per-division score entry grid. Organizers select an event and division, then enter scores for each athlete. Supports publishing/unpublishing division results. Multi-round scores flow through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]], which for `time-with-cap` workouts derives per-round cap status server-side from each round's encoded value against `workout.timeCap * 1000`, persists that status on `scoreRoundsTable`, preserves the summed total on `scoresTable.scoreValue` (instead of clamping to the cap), and threads `cappedRoundCount` into `computeSortKey` so the leaderboard tiebreaker honors "fewer capped rounds wins".

For **online competitions**: Shows a submissions overview with links to individual video verification pages at `/events/{eventId}/submissions/`. Each child sub-event is listed as its own row with submission counts and verification status; the parent event name is shown as contextual metadata alongside each child row. Events without `competition_events` rows (no submission window configured) are handled gracefully.

### Division Results Publish Gate

Controls whether scores for a given (event, division) pair are visible on the public leaderboard. Organizers toggle publish state per event-division from the results entry UI.

Publish state lives in `competitionsTable.settings.divisionResults[trackWorkoutId][divisionId].publishedAt` — an ISO timestamp when published, absent/null when in draft. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server/competition-leaderboard.ts#getCompetitionLeaderboard]] reads this shape and gates two flows with it:

1. Per-event leaderboard entries: inside the per-event loop, `if (!divisionPublishState?.publishedAt) continue` drops any (event, division) that isn't published.
2. Video visibility: the `isEventDivisionPublished` helper returns `false` for unpublished pairs so their videos don't leak.

Defaults: when `divisionResults` is absent entirely, online competitions treat everything as hidden (opt-in publishing) while in-person competitions show everything (backwards compat for gyms that never opted into the gate). Organizers can bulk-publish all divisions for an event from `QuickActionsDivisionResults`.

## Submission Windows

Manages time windows during which athletes can submit video evidence for online competitions.

- Registration questions editor (custom questions athletes answer during signup)
- Pending teammate invitations tab for team divisions
- Per-athlete deep link (name + "View" column) to the [[organizer-dashboard#Registrations (Athletes)#Athlete Detail Page]]

### Athlete Detail Page

At `/compete/organizer/{competitionId}/athletes/{registrationId}` — organizer-only editing surface covering every field on a single registration plus per-event scores or video submissions.

The route lives at [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId.tsx]] and composes section components under the `-components/` sibling folder. It hydrates from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn]], a single fetch that returns the registration, captain user, athlete team memberships, pending invites, registration questions + answers, waivers + signatures, competition events, and all linked video submissions / scores.

Sections and the mutations they call:
- **Registration info** — inline edit of `teamName` via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#updateRegistrationTeamNameFn]]; division change opens the shared `TransferDivisionDialog` which calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#transferRegistrationDivisionFn]]; registration removal calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#removeRegistrationFn]] and redirects back to the list.
- **Athlete profile (per member)** — first name, last name, email via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#updateAthleteUserProfileFn]]. Edits write directly to the `userTable` row, so this mutates the user's global profile, not a competition-scoped override.
- **Team members** (team divisions only) — active member list with remove-teammate ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#removeTeammateFromRegistrationFn]]); pending invites list with resend ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#resendTeamInviteAsOrganizerFn]]) and cancel ([[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#cancelPendingTeamInviteFn]]); "Add Teammate" dialog calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#addTeammateToRegistrationFn]] (inline invite + email send, bypasses the captain-only `INVITE_MEMBERS` check).
- **Registration answers** — per-question per-member inline edit via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#updateRegistrationAnswerFn]] (empty answer deletes the row).
- **Video submissions** (online competitions) — per competition event, shows each existing submission with an inline `VideoPlayerEmbed`, reuses `OrganizerVideoLinksEditor` for URL edits and `EnterScoreForm` for first-time score entry, and links to the full submission-verification page for score adjustments. Empty event slots offer a **ManualSubmissionDialog** that calls [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#createOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn]] (creates the submission and, when `videoIndex === 0`, the score in a single call). Delete goes through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts#deleteOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn]], which cascade-deletes the linked score row when the deleted submission was the score owner.
- **Scores** (in-person competitions) — per competition event, edit with [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#saveCompetitionScoreFn]] and delete with [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/competition-score-fns.ts#deleteCompetitionScoreFn]].

Why a dedicated org-side `resendTeamInviteAsOrganizerFn` exists: the captain-facing [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/registration-fns.ts#refreshCompetitionTeamInviteFn]] rejects non-captain callers and only allows refreshing *expired* invites. The organizer variant authorizes via `MANAGE_COMPETITIONS` on the organizing team and lets organizers resend any pending invite regardless of expiry.

1	import "server-only"
2	
3	/**
4	 * Organizer Athlete Detail Server Functions for TanStack Start
5	 *
6	 * Organizer-scoped mutations + fetch for a single competition registration.
7	 * All endpoints require MANAGE_COMPETITIONS on the organizing team (or site admin).
8	 */
9	// @lat: [[organizer-dashboard#Registrations (Athletes)]]
10	
11	import { createId } from "@paralleldrive/cuid2"
12	import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
13	import { and, asc, eq, inArray, isNull, ne } from "drizzle-orm"
14	import { z } from "zod"
15	import { getDb } from "@/db"
16	import {
17	  commercePurchaseTable,
18	  competitionEventsTable,
19	  competitionRegistrationAnswersTable,
20	  competitionRegistrationQuestionsTable,
21	  competitionRegistrationsTable,
22	  competitionsTable,
23	  createCompetitionRegistrationAnswerId,
24	  createTeamInvitationId,
25	  createVideoSubmissionId,
26	  INVITATION_STATUS,
27	  REGISTRATION_STATUS,
28	  ROLES_ENUM,
29	  SYSTEM_ROLES_ENUM,
30	  scalingLevelsTable,
31	  scoreRoundsTable,
32	  scoresTable,
33	  TEAM_PERMISSIONS,
34	  teamInvitationTable,
35	  teamMembershipTable,
36	  teamTable,
37	  userTable,
38	  videoSubmissionsTable,
39	  waiverSignaturesTable,
40	  waiversTable,
41	} from "@/db/schema"
42	import { trackWorkoutsTable } from "@/db/schemas/programming"
43	import { workouts } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
44	import { getEvlog } from "@/lib/evlog"
45	import {
46	  addRequestContextAttribute,
47	  logEntityCreated,
48	  logEntityDeleted,
49	  logEntityUpdated,
50	  logInfo,
51	  updateRequestContext,
52	} from "@/lib/logging"
53	import { requireVerifiedEmail } from "@/utils/auth"
54	import { saveCompetitionScoreFn } from "./competition-score-fns"
55	
56	// ============================================================================
57	// Helpers
58	// ============================================================================
59	
60	type Session = Awaited<ReturnType<typeof requireVerifiedEmail>>
61	
62	async function loadCompetitionForAuth(competitionId: string) {
63	  const db = getDb()
64	  const competition = await db.query.competitionsTable.findFirst({
65	    where: eq(competitionsTable.id, competitionId),
66	    columns: {
67	      id: true,
68	      name: true,
69	      slug: true,
70	      organizingTeamId: true,

content.text:
All five tasks complete. Here's what shipped:

**New route**: `/compete/organizer/$competitionId/athletes/$registrationId` — organizer's single-surface edit page.

**New server fns** (`src/server-fns/organizer-athlete-fns.ts`, all `MANAGE_COMPETITIONS`-gated):
- `getOrganizerAthleteDetailFn` — one fetch for the whole page
- `updateRegistrationTeamNameFn`, `updateAthleteUserProfileFn`, `updateRegistrationAnswerFn`
- `addTeammateToRegistrationFn`, `removeTeammateFromRegistrationFn`, `cancelPendingTeamInviteFn`, `resendTeamInviteAsOrganizerFn`
- `createOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn` (guards `videoIndex===0` on score), `deleteOrganizerVideoSubmissionFn` (cascade-deletes linked score)

**UI sections** (co-located under `athletes/-components/`): registration-info-card, athlete-profile-card, team-members-section, add-teammate-dialog, answers-section, waivers-section (read-only), video-submissions-section + event-submission-card + manual-submission-dialog (online), scores-section + event-score-card (in-person).

**List integration**: `athletes.tsx` moved to `athletes/index.tsx`. Athlete name is now a `<Link>` and a new "View" column (ArrowUpRight icon) appears before the dropdown; mobile cards have a "View details" link. Destructive dropdown actions preserved.

**Validation**: `pnpm type-check` clean, `biome check` on new files clean (4 pre-existing warnings in moved file), `lat check` passes, lat.md updated with an "Athlete Detail Page" subsection.

**Deferred**: multi-round scheme-aware editing on in-person scores and inline score adjustments on existing submissions both link out to the existing dedicated review page.

content.content:
185	The loader in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/workouts/$eventId.tsx#Route]] fetches video submissions for each child event in parallel via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getVideoSubmissionFn]]. Each child renders its own [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/video-submission-form.tsx#VideoSubmissionForm]] with the child's `trackWorkoutId`. Team divisions get the same multi-video slot behavior per sub-event. The parent event itself has no submission form when children exist — all scoring is per sub-event.
186	
187	The [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/athlete-score-submission-panel.tsx#AthleteScoreSubmissionPanel]] also respects this hierarchy. For parents with children, it renders a compact parent header with individual child rows beneath it. Submission data is fetched for child IDs (not the parent), and child rows link to the parent event page where per-sub-event forms live. The panel filters events by event-division mappings: if a parent is mapped to specific divisions and the selected division isn't included, the entire group (parent + children) is excluded. Children without explicit mappings inherit their parent's mapping visibility. On mobile the panel renders above the registration sidebar; on desktop it appears in the main content column.
188	
189	### Captain-Only Submission
190	
191	For team divisions (teamSize > 1), only the team captain can submit videos and scores. Non-captain team members see a read-only view of the team's submissions.
192	
193	Individual athletes (teamSize = 1) are always treated as their own captain. Both [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getAthleteDivisionSubmissionsFn]] and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#submitVideoFn]] enforce this — the panel sets `canSubmit = false` for non-captains so they never see submit/edit actions.
194	
195	Reviewers bypass the captain restriction through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#updateSubmissionVideoUrlFn]] (see [[lat.md/domain#Domain Model#Video Submissions#Reviewer Video Link Editor]]): organizers and score-input volunteers can edit existing links and create new rows for unfilled slots, and the server still attributes those inserts to `registration.captainUserId` (falling back to `registration.userId` for solo) so the team's submissions stay grouped under a single athlete id for downstream queries like `getOrganizerSubmissionsFn`.
196	
197	### Multiple Videos per Team
198	
199	Team divisions allow up to `teamSize` video submissions per event, tracked via a `videoIndex` column on `videoSubmissionsTable`.
200	
201	The unique constraint is `(registrationId, trackWorkoutId, videoIndex)`. Videos are optional — teams can submit fewer than `teamSize`. The score is submitted once per team (tied to the captain's userId): the form sends the score (and `roundScores`) only with the first slot's `submitVideoFn` call (`videoIndex === 0`); subsequent teammate slots arrive with no score by design, and the server's score-required check matches that contract by gating on `videoIndex === 0`.
202	
203	Incomplete team submissions are surfaced to organizers in two places. The list at [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/events/$eventId/submissions/index.tsx]] compares the group's `videoCount` to `division.teamSize` and renders an amber `videoCount/teamSize videos` warning badge on the row. The review detail's [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/organizer-video-links-editor.tsx#OrganizerVideoLinksEditor]] then renders blank input fields for the unsubmitted slots so a reviewer can fill them in via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#updateSubmissionVideoUrlFn]] — which respects the `(registrationId, trackWorkoutId, videoIndex)` unique constraint by re-checking for an existing row before inserting.
204	
205	### Round Breakdown Display
206	
207	Multi-round scores are displayed with a total followed by each round's time, so athletes and organizers can see how the aggregate was built.
208	
209	[[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getVideoSubmissionFn]] and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getOrganizerSubmissionDetailFn]] both join `scoreRoundsTable` when `workout.roundsToScore > 1` and return `roundScores: Array<{roundNumber, value, displayScore, status}>` on the returned score. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/video-submission-preview.tsx#VideoSubmissionPreview]] (athlete-facing) and the Claimed Score card in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/events/$eventId/submissions/$submissionId.tsx]] (organizer-facing) render the rounds under the total when `roundScores.length > 1`, each row tagged with a "Cap" badge if `status === "cap"`. Single-round workouts render unchanged.
210	
211	The organizer submission **list** at [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/events/$eventId/submissions/index.tsx]] also surfaces the breakdown via [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getOrganizerSubmissionsFn]]: it pulls workout `roundsToScore`/`timeCap`, fetches `scoreRoundsTable` once for every score on a multi-round event, and renders a per-row inline summary (`R1 4:30 · R2 CAP (12:00)` plus an amber `N/M cap` indicator). The Claimed Score column also reuses [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/lib/scoring/format/score.ts#formatScore]] with `secondaryValue` so single-round capped reps render as `CAP (N reps)` instead of just the cap time.
212	
213	The list surfaces missing partner uploads alongside the athlete name: `getOrganizerSubmissionsFn` selects `scalingLevelsTable.teamSize` into `division.teamSize`, the list groups rows by `registrationId` and computes `videoCount` per group, and when `teamSize > 1 && videoCount < teamSize` the row renders an amber `AlertTriangle` badge (`1/2 videos`) instead of the neutral "N videos" badge. This lets organizers spot incomplete team submissions at a glance before opening the review detail, where [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/organizer-video-links-editor.tsx#OrganizerVideoLinksEditor]] can fill in the unsubmitted slot.
214	
215	After a successful submit, [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/video-submission-form.tsx#VideoSubmissionForm]] computes the optimistic preview aggregate locally using `encodeRounds` + `decodeScore` with the workout's `scoreType`, so the athlete sees the correct total (e.g. `41:00.000` for a partner sum workout) immediately rather than a "R1 + R2" concatenation. Per-round cap state is inferred client-side from `encodedRound >= timeCap * 1000` to mirror the server derivation — once the loader refetches, the server-computed values take over.
216	
217	### Grouped Review UX
218	
219	Organizers review multi-video team submissions in a tabbed UI with aggregated no-rep tallies.
220	
221	The submission list groups videos by registration into a single row with a badge. The server computes `registrationAllReviewed` from the full unfiltered submission set so grouped rows show correct review status even when a status filter is active. The review detail page uses [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getSiblingSubmissionsFn]] to fetch sibling videos and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/review-note-fns.ts#getReviewNotesForRegistrationFn]] to aggregate review notes across all videos. Both functions scope queries to the competition by joining through `competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId` to prevent cross-competition data access. Per-video notes feed into a shared movement tally. When a video submission has no associated score yet, the sidebar renders [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/enter-score-form.tsx#EnterScoreForm]] in place of the verification controls so the reviewer can create the missing score directly. Video URLs rendered in the organizer list are guarded with `isSafeUrl` to prevent `javascript:` scheme injection. Tab labels use generic role names ("Captain", "Teammate 1", etc.) derived from `videoIndex` rather than athlete names, since the captain submits all team videos and the `userId` on each row is always the captain's.
222	
223	### Reviewer Video Link Editor
224	
225	Both review surfaces let an organizer or volunteer correct a broken athlete-submitted link — or fill in a partner slot the captain never uploaded for — without impersonating the athlete.
226	
227	Rendered directly under the Verification Controls / Adjust Score card on [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/review/$eventId/$submissionId.tsx]] and [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/events/$eventId/submissions/$submissionId.tsx]], [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/organizer-video-links-editor.tsx#OrganizerVideoLinksEditor]] mirrors the partner-video layout from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/video-submission-form.tsx#VideoSubmissionForm]]: it renders `teamSize` slots keyed by `videoIndex` (labeled "Captain" / "Teammate N" for teams, "Video URL" for solo), with existing submissions populated by `videoIndex` and unfilled indexes rendered as blank [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/ui/video-url-input.tsx]] fields tagged with a "Not submitted" badge. Save is disabled until the URL is both dirty and validates; on success the component calls `router.invalidate()` so the embedded video player, notes panel, and sibling list refetch. [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#getSiblingSubmissionsFn]] also returns `teamSize`, `registrationId`, `trackWorkoutId`, and `captainUserId` so the editor has the context it needs to create new rows.
228	
229	Writes go through [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/video-submission-fns.ts#updateSubmissionVideoUrlFn]], which accepts either `submissionId` (update existing row) or `registrationId + trackWorkoutId + videoIndex` (insert a new row, attributed to the captain's `userId` to group with the captain's other submissions). It guards with `requireSubmissionReviewAccess`, scopes every write through `competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId` to prevent cross-competition access, and re-checks for an existing slot row before inserting to tolerate a race where another reviewer just created the same slot.
230	
231	### Score Adjustments
232	
233	Reviewers can adjust a submitted score with or without assigning a penalty. Penalty classification is optional on adjust; the score change is the primary action.
234	
235	The Verification Controls card surfaces three top-level actions: Verify, Adjust Score, and Mark Invalid. The Adjust Score form exposes a `Penalty (optional)` radio with None / Minor / Major, defaulting to None. When None is selected the client omits `penaltyType` from [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/server-fns/submission-verification-fns.ts#verifySubmissionScoreFn]], which stores the adjustment with `verificationStatus = "adjusted"`, null penalty fields on `scoresTable`, and sets the video `reviewStatus` to `"adjusted"` rather than `"penalized"`. Minor/Major selections keep the original penalty flow with the denormalized `penaltyType`/`penaltyPercentage` columns populated. Both the organizer surface [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/organizer/$competitionId/events/$eventId/submissions/$submissionId.tsx]] and the volunteer surface [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/routes/compete/$slug/review/$eventId/$submissionId.tsx]] share this form.
236	
237	The adjust inputs mirror the athlete submission form: `parseScore` validates entries and shows a "Parsed as" preview, cap status is derived automatically from parsed time vs `event.workout.timeCap` (no manual Status picker), the reps-at-cap secondary input appears only when the cap is auto-hit, and a tiebreak input renders when `event.workout.tiebreakScheme` is set (POSTed as `tieBreakScore`). Shared input-hint helpers live in [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/score-entry-helpers.ts]] so `enter-score-form.tsx` and both review surfaces stay consistent. The "Athlete claimed" readout also appends the decoded tiebreak (`· TB: <value>`) whenever the workout defines a `tiebreakScheme`; when the athlete didn't enter one, the value renders as `—` so reviewers can tell at a glance that the tiebreak was skipped rather than assume none was required.
238	
239	The Adjust Score form's penalty-tiebreak state is seeded from `submission.score.tiebreakValue` (the decoded display string) rather than the empty string, so editing other fields without touching the tiebreak input preserves the existing value. Without that seed, the server would receive `tieBreakScore: undefined`, fail the `if (data.tieBreakScore && score.tiebreakScheme)` truthiness check, and persist `tiebreakValue: null`, silently erasing a previously-entered tiebreak.
240	
241	### Manual Score Entry
242	
243	When an athlete uploads a video without filling in the score field, the sidebar renders [[apps/wodsmith-start/src/components/compete/enter-score-form.tsx#EnterScoreForm]] instead of the placeholder, letting the reviewer create the missing score in one step.
244
